items.label: About ZYNQ.peer
items.content:
# ZYNQ.peer

`ZYNQ.peer` is a lightweight, event-driven WebRTC wrapper built on top of PeerJS. It enables real-time peer-to-peer communication including messaging, video/audio streaming, and session management with minimal setup.

It is designed for simplicity while still offering powerful control over connections, media streams, and lifecycle handling.

---

### Core Capabilities

* **Peer-to-Peer Messaging** — Send and receive structured messages instantly.
* **WebRTC Media Streaming** — Built-in support for video/audio calls.
* **Session Management** — Automatically tracks active peers.
* **Event System** — Reactive architecture using `on()` listeners.
* **Auto Heartbeat** — Detects disconnects and cleans sessions.
* **Zero Config Start** — Works out of the box with minimal setup.

---

### Features Overview

* Automatic PeerJS loading (CDN fallback)
* Real-time bitrate monitoring
* Smart reconnect / timeout handling
* Stream lifecycle management
* Broadcast and direct messaging
* Fully asynchronous API

---

### Installation

#### Standard Script
```html
<script src="your-zynq-peer.js"></script>
```

#### PeerJS (optional manual include)
```html
<script src="https://unpkg.com/peerjs@1.5.4/dist/peerjs.min.js"></script>
```

items.label: Quick Start
Start Options
items.content:
# Start Options

You can configure the peer instance during startup.

### Example
```javascript
await ZYNQ.peer.start({
  host: 'your-server.com',
  port: 443,
  secure: true
});
```

These options are passed directly to the PeerJS constructor.

---

### Debug Level

```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.setDebug(2);
```

| Level | Description |
|------|------------|
| 0 | Silent |
| 1 | Basic logs |
| 2 | Verbose |
| 3 | Full debug |

---

### Auto Start

```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.setAutoStart(true);
```

Controls whether internal logic loops automatically start.

items.label: Properties
items.content:
# Properties

### Main Properties

| Property | Type | Description |
|---------|------|------------|
| `peer` | Object | PeerJS instance |
| `sessions` | Map | Active peer sessions |
| `localStream` | MediaStream | Current local media stream |
| `ready` | Boolean | Whether peer is ready |
| `myId` | String | Your peer ID |

---

### Example
```javascript
console.log(ZYNQ.peer.getMyId());
console.log(ZYNQ.peer.getActivePeers());
```

items.label: Methods
items.content:
# Methods

### Connection Methods

* `start(options)` → Initialize peer
* `connect(peerId, config)` → Connect to peer
* `disconnect(peerId)` → Disconnect session
* `destroy()` → Destroy entire instance

---

### Messaging

* `sendTo(peerId, message)` → Send message
* `broadcast(message)` → Send to all peers

---

### Media

* Automatically handled via connection negotiation
* Uses `getUserMedia`

---

### Utility

* `getMyId()` → Return your peer ID
* `getActivePeers()` → List connected peers
* `setDebug(level)` → Set debug level
* `setAutoStart(bool)` → Enable/disable auto logic loop

items.label: Event System
items.content:
# Events

ZYNQ.peer uses an event-driven system.

### Register Event
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('event', callback);
```

### Remove Event
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.off('event', callback);
```

---

### Available Events

| Event | Description |
|------|------------|
| `ready` | Peer initialized |
| `invite` | Incoming connection request |
| `joined` | Peer joined session |
| `left` | Peer disconnected |
| `message` | Message received |
| `remote-stream` | Remote media stream |
| `local-stream` | Local media stream ready |
| `local-stream-ended` | Local stream stopped |
| `bitrate` | Live bitrate update |
| `rejected` | Connection rejected |
| `error` | Error occurred |
| `destroyed` | Instance destroyed |

---

### Example
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('invite', ({ id, accept, reject }) => {
  accept();
});
```

items.label: Media Configuration
items.content:
# Media Configuration

When connecting, you can define media settings.

### Example
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.connect('peer-id', {
  video: true,
  audio: true,
  resolution: 720,
  fps: 30
});
```

---

### Options

| Option | Type | Description |
|-------|------|------------|
| `video` | Boolean | Enable video |
| `audio` | Boolean | Enable audio |
| `resolution` | Number | 720 or 1080 |
| `fps` | Number | Frames per second |

---

### Stream Events

```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('remote-stream', ({ peerId, stream }) => {
  video.srcObject = stream;
});
```

items.label: Session Lifecycle
items.content:
# Session Lifecycle

Each connected peer is stored as a session.

### Session Object

```javascript
{
  conn,
  call,
  config,
  lastSeen,
  lastBytes,
  status
}
```

---

### Lifecycle Flow

1. Connect request (`REQ`)
2. Accept (`ACC`) or reject (`REJ`)
3. Media call starts
4. Heartbeat keeps connection alive
5. Disconnect on timeout or manual

---

### Timeout Handling

Peers are automatically disconnected after ~12 seconds of inactivity.

items.label: Heartbeat System
items.content:
# Heartbeat System

ZYNQ.peer runs an internal loop every 2 seconds.

### Responsibilities

* Sends `PING` messages
* Expects `PONG` responses
* Tracks last activity time
* Calculates bitrate
* Disconnects inactive peers

---

### Bitrate Monitoring

```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.on('bitrate', ({ peerId, kbps }) => {
  console.log(`${peerId}: ${kbps} kbps`);
});
```

items.label: Cleanup & Destroy
items.content:
# Cleanup & Destroy

### Destroy Instance
```javascript
ZYNQ.peer.destroy();
```

---

### What Happens

* All sessions disconnected
* Media tracks stopped
* PeerJS instance destroyed
* Events cleared
* Internal loop stopped

---

### Auto Cleanup

Local media stream is automatically stopped when no sessions remain.
